title: Amphineurion velutinum
intro: Amphineurion velutinum, commonly known as the velvety chain-fern or Amphineurion, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Amphineurion velutinum include lignans, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Amphineurion velutinum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of capsules, tinctures, and topical creams to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
